sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient is a 45-year-old male with a history of diabetes type two and mild intermittent asthma who presents with a 3-week history of right elbow pain.
He reports that the pain started after increasing his tennis activity and describes it as a sharp, stabbing, shooting, and throbbing pain, rated 8/10 with movement and 3/10 at rest.
He has tried Tylenol and ibuprofen, but they do not provide relief.
He also mentions that he has not had any trauma or unusual pain patterns.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of diabetes type two, mild intermittent asthma, an allergy to penicillin, and a history of gallbladder removal 10 years ago.
Review of systems: The patient denies any fever or other systemic symptoms.
Current medications: Metformin 500mg twice a day, Flovent twice a day, Albuterol as needed.
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]

Physical exam: The patient has a grade 3/6 systolic ejection murmur, moderate lateral epicondylar tenderness of the right elbow, mild pain with passive range of motion of the right elbow, and mild pain with resisted extension of the right wrist.
The physical exam is otherwise normal.
Test Results: The patient's right elbow x-ray shows no acute fracture or other bony abnormality, no malalignment or joint effusion.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has acute lateral epicondylitis of the right elbow, also known as tennis elbow, likely due to overuse of the elbow from increased tennis activity.
Problem list: Acute lateral epicondylitis of the right elbow; diabetes type two; mild intermittent asthma.
Plan:
I recommended that the patient rest his right arm as much as possible, stop tennis for 4-6 weeks, and avoid repetitive stress on the right arm.
I prescribed ice for the right elbow for 20 minutes, 3 times a day, and a brace for the right forearm.
I also wrote a prescription for Ultram 50mg once a day for pain relief.
I will see the patient in 4 weeks for a follow-up.
Follow-up: The patient will return in 4 weeks for a follow-up appointment.
